seo.title: Break-even ROAS Explained: Formula, Examples & Calculator
seo.description: Learn what break-even ROAS means, how to calculate it from margin and AOV, and why platform ROAS can mislead. Includes ecommerce and lead gen examples.

headline: Break-even ROAS Explained: What It Is and How to Calculate It
intro: Break-even ROAS is the minimum return on ad spend where your campaign stops losing money after product costs — not the revenue number your ad platform shows by default.

blocks.text: ROAS (return on ad spend) is revenue divided by ad spend. Break-even ROAS is the ROAS where contribution margin equals ad spend — profit after product costs is zero. Any ROAS below that threshold means you lose money on each conversion, even when revenue looks healthy.

blocks.title: Key insight:
blocks.text: Platform ROAS uses gross revenue. Break-even ROAS uses contribution margin. They are not the same number.
First find contribution per conversion: value times gross margin percent, minus any fixed cost per order or per lead.
With no fixed costs, this simplifies to 1 ÷ margin. Fifty percent margin means 2.0× break-even ROAS. Twenty-five percent margin means 4.0×.
AOV $90, gross margin 50%, shipping and fees $6 per order.

items: At 2.0× platform ROAS, the campaign loses money despite $180 revenue on $90 spend

blocks.before: Run your own numbers in the

sections.heading: Lead gen: use expected revenue per lead

blocks.text: For lead gen, value is expected revenue per lead: customer or deal value multiplied by close rate. A $5,000 deal at 15% close rate has $750 expected value per lead. Apply the same formula to that value.
Conversion rate on the landing page does not change break-even ROAS — only margin and value per conversion matter.

faq.question: What is break-even ROAS?
faq.answer: Break-even ROAS is the minimum ROAS where ad spend equals contribution margin per conversion. Below it, campaigns lose money after product or fulfillment costs.

faq.question: How do I calculate break-even ROAS?
faq.answer: Divide value (AOV or expected revenue per lead) by contribution per conversion. Contribution equals value times margin percent minus fixed costs.

faq.question: Does conversion rate affect break-even ROAS?
faq.answer: No. Conversion rate affects max CPC and volume, but break-even ROAS depends only on margin and value per conversion.

faq.question: Is break-even ROAS the same as target ROAS?
faq.answer: Break-even ROAS is your floor. Target ROAS in ad platforms should be at or above this number — often higher if you want profit, not just break-even.
